findings.title: Rebalance initialization can be seized if deployment is non-atomic or proxy is left uninitialized

best_agent_match.title: Upgradeable rebalance contract is takeover-prone if initialization is not atomic

rejected_candidates.title: Front-Running Attack: initialize() Can Be Front-Run to Steal All Minted Tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: For clones, `LamboFactory` performs `clone` and `initialize` in the same transaction flow; an attacker cannot call `initialize` on the just-created clone before factory initialization.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Drain All vETH From The Protocol Via updateFactory
rejected_candidates.reason: This is privileged-owner behavior; README explicitly states owner roles are trusted, so this is not a distinct trustless vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited Token Minting via createLaunchPad with Zero Validation
rejected_candidates.reason: Creating new quote tokens per launchpad is intended factory behavior; claim relies on trusted-owner whitelist management rather than an unintended permission bypass.

rejected_candidates.title: No Slippage Protection Results in Potential Total Loss of Funds
rejected_candidates.reason: `buyQuote`/`sellQuote` do enforce `minReturn`; users setting `minReturn=0` is caller choice, not a contract-side bypass.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee Calculation Precision Loss Favors Protocol Over Users
rejected_candidates.reason: Integer-division rounding dust is expected in Solidity fee math and does not evidence a meaningful systematic overcharge exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: rebalance ignores `amountOut` and executes OKX swaps with `minReturn = 0`
rejected_candidates.reason: Although `amountOut` is unused and swap minReturn is zero, `rebalance` reverts unless net WETH balance increases (`profit > 0`), preventing protocol-loss execution.

rejected_candidates.title: No Slippage Protection in Rebalance Function
rejected_candidates.reason: Same core issue as above: execution is profit-gated and non-profitable outcomes revert, so reported total-loss claim is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on Rebalance Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: `extractProfit` is `onlyOwner`; open `rebalance` entrypoint does not grant fund withdrawal and is consistent with keeper-style triggering.

rejected_candidates.title: Approval Reset Allows Potential Race Condition
rejected_candidates.reason: No concrete exploit path shown; approvals are to fixed integration addresses for WETH/vETH flows, and overwriting allowance here is not a standalone vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Unverified underlyingToken Can Break VirtualToken
rejected_candidates.reason: `underlyingToken` is immutable constructor configuration by deployer/trusted owner; this is deployment misconfiguration risk, not an in-protocol exploit vector.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy Protection on sellQuote Uses msg.sender.call Without Reentrancy Guard
rejected_candidates.reason: `sellQuote` is protected by `nonReentrant`; callback-based reentry into guarded paths is blocked.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded external addresses create chain-deployment fund-loss risk
rejected_candidates.reason: Project context specifies Ethereum-only deployment; hardcoded mainnet integration addresses are intentional chain-locking in this codebase rather than a reportable bug by themselves.
